threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest Tube station like it's a shortcut, oblivious to the bumpy bus routes and that cycling is basically a rite of passage.
Caught the 198 last night, driver stopped for a couple arguing outside the bus. Ended up playing therapist. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I stumbled upon an open-air flea market at Maybachufer, where the aroma of grilled corn mixed with distant techno beats—that’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a park where the public lawn doubles as an open-air gym, complete with makeshift weights and yoga classes at dawn.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the local taco trucks but still get baffled by Muni routes after a rainy week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a saxophone play an entire set at the bus stop. Muni was late, but everyone stayed to listen. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are in fancy restaurants; the real gems are in the tiny bars where the locals squeeze in for a quick caña.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their constant map-checking at every corner, like they’re searching for lost treasure in plain sight.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'the Tube' like it's a proper name, but everyone just calls it 'the train' or 'the bus' round here.
Last weekend, I spotted a group on the N35 arguing over who could sing the loudest. The driver just sighed and turned up the radio.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids using a trampoline on the sidewalk while their parents sipped coffee at the nearby Späti. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stops at 1 AM, but the people keep partying in the tunnels? It’s like an underground dance party waiting to happen.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART exit after the latest bang and smoke incident—only tourists look puzzled.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in the Tenderloin using his skateboard to tow a Muni bus that had stalled. Only in San Francisco do you get that kind of creativity in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they're 'experiencing local life' in the plaça, but the real heart of Gràcia is in the hidden corners, where the neighborhood's soul breathes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to navigate the metro card machines—watch for the puzzled expressions and unscanned tickets!
